reforms.description: The Canadian government has implemented tax incentives and grants to stimulate private sector R&D spending while reviewing its major R&D support programme, the Scientific Research and Experimental Development tax incentive, to enhance its effectiveness. Additionally, proposals have been outlined to link various economic development instruments to improve innovation performance across sectors, particularly for small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance private sector R&D investment and innovation, which are critical for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax incentives and grants have been put into effect, and the strategy has been officially adopted.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of tax incentives and a comprehensive strategy for innovation represents a significant overhaul of the R&D support system.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government's major R&D support programme, the Scientific Research and Experimental Development tax incentive, came under review in the last Budget.

reforms.description: The analysis suggests that Canadian industry would benefit from increased private sector spending and performance in R&D, indicating a need for public policies to stimulate such investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Stimulating private sector R&D investment is essential for enhancing overall economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The analysis indicates a recognized need for policy adjustments to enhance R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the analysis highlights a significant need, it does not represent a direct policy change.
reforms.source_quote: An obvious question raised by the analysis above is what restrains Canadian firms from spending more on - and performing more - R&D if there are substantial net benefits to doing so.

reforms.description: The Canadian government plans to enhance the efficiency of the transportation network by revising outdated regulations and investing in the Canadian Network for the Advancement of Research, Industry and Education (CANARIE) to create a high-speed experimental communications network aimed at helping innovative companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving transportation efficiency and investing in research infrastructure are vital for supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans for investment and regulatory changes have been publicly stated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The investment in CANARIE and regulatory revisions represent significant but not transformative changes.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to improve the efficiency of Canada's transportation network by removing or revising outmoded regulations and reducing or eliminating subsidies.

reforms.description: The Canadian government presented a comprehensive industrial strategy titled 'Building a More Innovative Economy' aimed at increasing international competitiveness and fostering innovation, particularly in SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance competitiveness and innovation, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been officially adopted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant structural change in how the government approaches industrial policy.
reforms.source_quote: In November 1994, the Government of Canada presented in a report entitled Building a More Innovative Economy (BMIE) a comprehensive industrial strategy designed to increase Canada's international competitiveness.
